name: Green Beans with Water Chestnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the green beans and water chestnuts ahead of time, but cook just before mealtime.
2. Serve tender-crisp and piping hot.
3. Thin, fresh asparagus spears can be substituted for green beans.
4. Wash and drain the green beans. French-cut them in half lengthwise; set aside.
5. Combine chicken broth, ginger root, and garlic in a frypan. Bring to a boil.
6. Add green beans and water chestnuts. Sprinkle with soy sauce.
7. Cover tightly and steam for 7 to 8 minutes until tender-crisp.
8. If moisture has not dried off, remove the cover, raise heat briefly, and shake the pan to evaporate remaining liquid.
9. Season to taste with pepper.
3/4 cup serving - 44 calories, 1 fruits & veggie choice, 9 grams carbohydrate, 2 grams protein
Source: Choice Cooking, Canadian Diabetes Association 1986
Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93
